Logical Reasoning Puzzles require analyzing the given  
information and picking the definite information first and  
arranging them as required. The student needs to consider  
all the possible cases while solving the puzzle. Reasoning  
puzzles are mostly based on Floor, Box, Blood Relations,  
Arrangements, etc.  
Steps to Solve Logical Puzzles  
(i)  
The first few sentences of the puzzle give you the basic  
information that is required to know the general idea of the  
given puzzle.  
(ii) Select the information that is definite from given information  
and write down all the certain possibilities.  
(iii) Then use the secondary statements or the indefinite  
statement to eliminate the possibilities in order to get the  
desired answer.  
There are four types of Puzzle:  
Type 1.  
Floor Puzzle  
In this type of puzzle, information of people and  
their flats or offices on different floors of a building  
is given. We need to arrange the information  
accordingly to solve such problems.  
Note: Alives x floors above Bmeans there is only (x – 1) floor  
between A and B and A lives above B.  
Directions (1–5): Study the given information carefully and  
answer the following questions:  
There are seven persons i.e. A, B, C, D, E, F and G who live in a  
seven-storey building such that the ground floor of the building  
is marked as 1, and the floor above it is marked as 2 and so on till  
the topmost floor which is marked as 7. Persons who are named  
with consecutive letters do not live on adjacent floors. B does not  
live on the floor adjacent to A and C. Two persons live between  
A and C. E does not live immediately below A. A does not live  
below C. Only one person lives between B and D.  
Solution.  
1.  
Two persons live betweenAand C.Adoes not live below C.  
Only one person lives between B and D.

2.  
E does not live immediately below A.

Example 1. Who among the following lives on 2nd floor?  
Solution. As shown in the above table, E lives on 2nd floor.  
Example 2. Who among the following lives immediately below  
the floor on which C lives?  
Solution. As shown in the above table, no one lives below the  
floor on which C lives.  
Example 3. How many people live above the floor on which G  
lives?  
Solution. As shown in the above table, 4 people live above the  
floor on which G lives.  
Example 4. On which of the following floor does B live?  
Solution. As shown in the above table, B lives on 7th floor.  
Example 5. Who lives on the 6th Floor?  
Solution. As shown in the above table, F lives on the 6th Floor.  
Type 2.  
Box Based Puzzle  
In these type of puzzles, information of position of  
boxes in a stack is given. We need to arrange the  
information accordingly to answer the following  
questions.  
Directions (6–10): Study the following information carefully  
and answer the questions given below.  
Seven boxes i.e. M, N, O, P, Q, R and S are kept one above the  
other but not necessarily in the same order. Three boxes are kept  
between box Pand box M. Only one box is kept between Q and P.  
Two boxes are kept between N and O. Only one box is kept  
between boxes M and O. Box N and M are not kept adjacent to  
each other. Box R is kept above box S, which is kept above box Q.  
Solution.  
1.  
Two boxes are kept between N and O. Only one box is kept  
between box M and O. Box N and M are not kept adjacent  
to each other.  
4-1  
Case 1  
Box  
M
Case 2  
Box  
N
O
O
N
M
2.  
Three boxes are kept between box P and box M. Only one  
box is kept between box Q and P. Box R is kept above box  
S, which is kept above box Q. Hence, case 2 gets eliminated.  
Case 1  
Box  
M

Example 6. Which box is kept at the bottom?  
Solution. As shown in the above table, box Q is kept at the  
bottom.  
Example 7. How many boxes are kept between R and P?  
Solution. As shown in the above table, 2 boxes are kept between  
R and P.  
Example 8. How many boxes are kept below box N?  
Solution. As shown in the above table, only one box is kept  
below box N.  
Example 9. If box M is related to box R, similarly box N is  
related to box Q, then following the same pattern box S is related  
to?  
Solution. As shown in the above table, box M and N are kept  
immediately above R and Q respectively. Similarly, box S is kept  
immediately above P.  
Example 10. Which box is kept immediately above box O?  
Solution. As shown in the above table, box R is kept immediately  
above box O.  
Type 3.  
Scheduling Puzzle  
There are four types in scheduling puzzle i.e. Day  
based, Month based, year based and month & date  
based.  
(1)  
Day Based  
In this type of puzzle, data related to days are given. We need to  
arrange the given data and answer following questions.  
Example 11. Six teachers i.e. A, B, C, D, E, and F have their  
lectures on six different days of the week starting from Monday to  
Saturday but not necessarily in the same order. Only two teachers  
have lectures beforeA. Two teachers have lecture between B and  
F. D has his lecture after C but not on Saturday. B has his lecture  
after D. Who among the following has a lecture on Thursday?  
Solution.  
(1) Only two teachers have lecture beforeA. Two teachers have  
lecture between B and F.  
Case 1  
Person  
F/B  
Case 2  
Person  
Days  
Monday  
Tuesday  
Wednesday  
Thursday  
Friday  
B/F  
A
A
B/F  
F/B  
Saturday  
(2) D has his lecture after C but not on Saturday. B has his  
lecture after D. Hence, case 1 gets eliminated. (As in case 1,  
B has his lecture before D.)  
Case 2  
Days  
Monday  
Person  
C
F
Tuesday  
Wednesday  
Thursday  
Friday  
A
D
B
E
Saturday  
Hence, D has a lecture on Thursday.  
Example 12. Six friends M, N, O, P, Q and R attend lecture  
at two different times of the day i.e. 10 a.m. or 4 p.m. on three  
different days of the week i.e. Tuesday, Wednesday and Friday  
but not necessarily in the same order. Two persons attend the  
lecture between R and Q, who attend the lecture in the morning.  
N attends the lecture immediately before Q. M and P attend the  
lecture at the same time but P is not the first person to attend the  
lecture. O attends the lecture after P. Who among the following  
attends the lecture on Wednesday at 10 a.m.?  
Solution. Two persons attend the lecture between R and Q,  
who attends the lecture in the morning. N attends the lecture  
immediately before Q.  
Case 1  
Case 2

M and P attend the lecture on the same time but P is not the  
first person to attend the lecture. O attends the lecture after P.  
(As in case 1 P attends the lecture immediately after O.) Hence,  
Case 1 gets eliminated.  
Case 2

10 a.m.

Hence, P attends lecture on Wednesday at 10 a.m.  
(2)  
Month Based  
In this type of puzzle, data related to months are given. We need  
to arrange the given data and answer the following questions.  
Example 13. There are six persons i.e. P, Q, R, S, T, and U who  
were born in six different months i.e. January, March, April,  
May, July and September of the same year but not necessarily  
in the same order. Only three persons were born between T and  
U. Neither T nor U was born in the month of January. Only one  
person was born between S and U. P was born before Q but after  
R. U was not born before Q. Who was born in the month ofApril?  
Solution.  
(1) Only three persons were born between T and U. Neither T  
nor U was born in the month of January. Only one person  
was born between S and U.  
Case 1  
Person  
Case 2  
Person  
Months  
January (31)  
March (31)  
April (30)  
May (31)  
T
S
U
S
July (31)  
September (30)  
U
T
(2) P was born before Q but after R. U was not born before Q.  
Hence, case 2 gets eliminated. (As in case 2, U was born in  
the month of March and Q was born in the month of July.)  
Case 1  
Months  
January (31)  
Person  
R
T
P
March (31)  
April (30)  
May (31)  
S
July (31)  
Q
U
September (30)  
Hence, P was born in April.  
(3)  
Year Based  
In this type of puzzle, some persons with data related to them in  
years are given. We need to arrange the given data accordingly.  
Example 14. Six persons namely M, N, O, P, Q and R were  
born on the same date and in the same month of the different year  
viz. 1984, 1988, 1997, 2001, 2010 and 2012 but not necessarily  
in same order. Ages are to be calculated from the base year  
2021. Two persons were born between N and Q. O was born  
either immediately before or immediately after N. Two persons  
were born between O and R. P is not older than M. P is not the  
youngest.Q is the second oldest.  
Who among the following is the second youngest person?  
(a) N  
(b) R  
(c) Q  
(d) P  
Solution. (a) (1) Q is the second oldest. Two persons were  
born between N and Q. O was born either immediately before or  
immediately after N.  
Case 1  
Person  
Case 2  
Person

1988

2001  
2010  
2012

N
O
(2) Two persons were born between O and R. P is not older than  
M. P is not the youngest. Hence, Case 1 gets eliminated.  
Case 2  
Year  
1984  
1988  
1997  
2001  
2010  
2012  
Person  
M
Q
R
P
N
O
Hence, N is the second youngest person.  
Example 15. Seven persons namely M, N, O, P, Q, R and S were  
born on the same date and month of different yearsi.e. 1965, 1973,  
1981, 1987, 1991, 1999 and 2005 but not necessarily in the same  
order. Ages are to be calculated from the base year 2021. Only  
three persons were born between M and N. Not more than two  
persons were born after M. The number of persons born before  
Q is same as the number of persons born after R. R was born  
before Q. R was not born immediately before N. O was born  
before P but after S. N is not the oldest among all.  
Who among the following was born in the year 1991?  
(a) R  
(b) Q  
(c) M  
(d) P  
Solution. (b) (1) Only three persons were born between M  
and N. Not more than two persons were born after M. N is not  
the oldest among all.  
Case 1  
Person  
Case 2

(2) Number of persons born before Q is same as the number  
of persons born after R. R was born before Q. R was not born  
immediately before N. Hence, case 1 gets eliminated. O was born  
before P but after S.

Hence, Q was born in 1991.  
(4)  
Month and Date  
In this type of puzzle, data related to months and dates are given  
together. We need to arrange the given data accordingly.  
Directions (16–19): Study the following information  
carefully and answer the questions given below.  
There are eight friends i.e. A, B, C, D, E, F, G and H were  
born on either 12th or 27th of four different months of the same  
year among May, June, July and September but not necessarily  
in the same order. No two persons were born on the same date of  
same month. Two persons were born between D and E. F and B  
were born in the same month. A was born after B. Two persons  
were born between C and A. D was born on an even date in the  
month having less than 31 days. F and C were born on the same  
date. H was born before G but not on the same date as A. Only  
one person was born between C and E.  
Example 16. Who was born on 27th June?  
Example 17. Who born on 12th September?  
Example 18. How many people were born between F and H?  
Example 19. If G is related to C and D is related to H, then who  
among the following person is related to A?  
(16–19): Common Solution  
(1) Two persons were born between D and E. D was born on  
even date in the month having less than 31 days.  
Only one person was born between C and E.  
Case 1  
Case 2  
Month  
May (31)

(2) F and B were born in the same month. A was born after B.  
Two persons born between C and A. F and C were born on  
the same date. H was born before G but not on the same  
date as A. Hence, case 2 gets eliminated.

16. From the above table, H was born on 27th June.  
17. From the above table, G was born on 12th September.  
18. From the above table, only 1 person was born between F  
and H.  
19. G and C are born in same month. Similarly, D and H are  
born in same month.  
Hence, A is related to E as both are born in same month i.e.  
July.  
Type 4.  
Variable Puzzle  
In this type of puzzle, information of people and  
their likes–dislikes or other variables is provided  
and you need to arrange the given data to answer  
the following questions.  
Example 20. Five persons i.e. A, B, D, E, and C like different  
fruits. B likes Grapes. E likes either Kiwi or Guava. C likes either  
Guava or Mango. D likes either Mango or Apple. A likes either  
Kiwi or Mango. C does not like Mango. D likes which fruit?  
Solution.  
(1) B likes Grapes. E likes either Kiwi or Guava. C likes either  
Guava or Mango. D likes either Mango or Apple.  
Person  
Fruits  
A
B
C
D
E
Grapes  
Guava/Mango  
Mango/Apple  
Kiwi/Guava  
(2) A likes either Kiwi or Mango. C does not like Mango.  
Person  
Fruits  
Mango  
Grapes  
Guava  
Apple  
Kiwi  
A
B
C
D
E
Hence, D likes Apple.  
Type 5.  
Puzzles Based on Blood Relations  
In these types of question, we are given some information  
regarding a members of the family. We are required to use this  
information to obtain the family tree.  
Directions (21–23): Read the following information  
carefully answer the given questions.  
Six persons viz.A, B, C, D, E and F belong to the same family.  
Each of them has a different profession among Clerk, Teacher,  
Singer, Boxer, Painter and Lawyer. There are three generations  
and two married couples in the family. Teacher belongs to the  
first generation. A is married to the teacher. B is the brother-in-  
law of C. E’s mother is a lawyer. Boxer is the paternal uncle of  
the singer. F is the father of the painter. E is the only child of the  
painter.  
Example 21. How is C related to the singer?  
(a) Mother  
(b) Father  
(c) Mother-in-law  
(d) None of these  
Example 22. How is lawyer related to the painter?  
(a) Husband  
(b) Wife  
(c) Sister-in-law  
(d) None of these  
Example 23. Who among the following is the clerk?  
(a) A (b) F (c) D (d) E  
(21–23): Common Solution:  
F is the father of the painter. E is the only child of the painter.  
It means F belongs to the first generation. Teacher belongs to the  
first generation. A is married to the teacher. It means A belongs  
to the first generation. B is the brother-in-law of C who belongs  
to the second generation. E’s mother is a lawyer. It means, E’s  
father must be the painter, F must be the teacher.  
(Teacher)  
F
A
(Painter)  
(Lawyer)  
E
Boxer is the paternal uncle of the singer. It means, E must be  
the singer, and B must be the boxer and the brother of the painter.  
So, the family tree can be represented by the following figure:  
(Teacher)  
(Clerk)  
F
A
C
D
B
(Painter)  
(Boxer)  
(Lawyer)  
E
(Singer)  
21. (a) Hence, C is the mother of the singer.  
22. (b) Hence, the lawyer is the wife of the painter.  
23. (a) Hence, A is the clerk.
